category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Spring Quickstart Guide: Hello World endpoint
surface.selection_basis: The official Spring Boot project page labels the Spring Quickstart Guide Super quick and the Building an Application guide More general. The Quickstart is therefore the documented shortest general Spring Boot entry. It creates a web project with Spring Initializr, adds one endpoint, runs embedded Tomcat, and verifies a browser response.

alternatives_considered.name: Building an Application with Spring Boot
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Spring Boot project page labels this the more general route; it continues through controller, application bean inspection, endpoint, unit tests, integration tests, Actuator, and build artifacts.

alternatives_considered.name: Developing Your First Spring Boot Application
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The reference tutorial creates Maven or Gradle project files manually and continues through an executable JAR.

alternatives_considered.name: Spring Getting Started Guides
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are task-specific Hello World guides for REST, data, security, messaging, and other use cases rather than the platform's super-quick route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create and run the official Spring Boot Hello World endpoint locally and verify it from a browser.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no Spring Boot project.
A compatible JDK and IDE are not assumed; no Spring account, cloud, token, or billing is required.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Build a classic Hello World endpoint which any browser can connect to.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Generate a Spring Web project, add the documented /hello controller, run it with the generated Gradle wrapper, and receive Hello World from embedded Tomcat on localhost:8080.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Opening http://localhost:8080/hello returns Hello World! from the running application.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The guide's What you'll build names the Hello World endpoint, and Step 3 ends by opening /hello and receiving the friendly response before the Pop quiz and next guides.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The HTTP response is the first externally observable application result and the terminal state of the three-step super-quick guide. Query parameters and additional guides follow it.

prerequisites.requirement: A Java Development Kit, with the quickstart recommending BellSoft Liberica JDK 17 or 21.
An IDE or editor; examples include IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code with Spring Boot Extension Pack, or Eclipse with Spring Tools.
Browser and network access to start.spring.io and permission to download and unpack a ZIP.
Permission to run the generated Gradle wrapper, download build dependencies, write build files, and bind local port 8080.

details: The Spring Boot project page labels this the super-quick getting-started route.
primary_path.input: Intent to create a first Spring Boot application.
primary_path.success_signal: What you'll build and prerequisites are visible.

primary_path.action: Install or verify a compatible JDK.
details: The guide recommends JDK 17 or 21.
The tool must be available to the IDE and shell.
primary_path.success_signal: The IDE and generated Gradle wrapper can find Java.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or incompatible Java prevents project import or run.

details: The guide allows IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code with Spring Boot Extension Pack, Eclipse with Spring Tools, or another IDE.
primary_path.output: IDE capable of opening the generated project.
primary_path.success_signal: The project can be opened and Java edited.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: IDE-specific installation and plugin steps are linked externally, not decomposed.

details: Spring Initializr generates the project and dependencies.
primary_path.success_signal: Project settings and Dependencies are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Service or network unavailability blocks generation.

primary_path.action: Configure a Spring Boot web project.
details: Use the Initializr project selections suitable for the quickstart.
The guide does not enumerate every current Project, Language, Spring Boot version, metadata, packaging, or Java field.
primary_path.success_signal: A Java Spring Boot project compatible with the local JDK is configured.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incompatible language, Java, build-tool, or Boot choices can change code and run commands.

details: In current Spring guides this dependency is named Spring Web.
primary_path.success_signal: Web appears in the project's dependencies.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Searching or selecting a different web stack changes annotations and runtime.

details: Initializr assembles the configured Spring Boot project as a ZIP.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser begins downloading the ZIP.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Validation or generation errors prevent download.

details: The browser saves the Initializr archive locally.
primary_path.success_signal: The ZIP is present on disk.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Browser download controls or filesystem permissions can block it.

primary_path.action: Unpack the ZIP into a local folder.
details: The extracted folder contains the generated wrapper, build configuration, and source tree.
primary_path.success_signal: Project files are visible in the folder.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Archive or filesystem errors can prevent extraction.

primary_path.action: Open the extracted project in the IDE.
details: Allow the IDE and build tool to import the project if prompted.
primary_path.success_signal: src/main/java/com/example/demo and generated files are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong project root or failed dependency import can hide source or create errors.

details: Locate it under src/main/java/com/example/demo.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Different Initializr package metadata changes the generated directory and package name.

primary_path.action: Add the documented imports and controller annotation.
details: Import GetMapping, RequestParam, and RestController.
Keep SpringApplication and SpringBootApplication imports.
Add @RestController alongside @SpringBootApplication.
primary_path.output: Class configured as a REST controller.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing Spring Web or mismatched package/imports produce compilation errors.

primary_path.action: Add the /hello method and save the file.
details: Use @GetMapping("/hello").
Accept @RequestParam name with defaultValue World.
Return String.format("Hello %s!", name).
primary_path.success_signal: The source contains the documented method and compiles syntactically.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Typing, quoting, brace, import, or annotation errors fail the build.

primary_path.action: Open a terminal and change to the project folder.
details: Run the wrapper from the directory containing gradlew.
primary_path.success_signal: gradlew is present in the current directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Using the wrong folder results in a missing wrapper.

primary_path.action: Build and run the application with the generated Gradle wrapper.
details: On macOS or Linux, run ./gradlew bootRun.
On Windows, run .\gradlew.bat bootRun.
primary_path.input: Project source, JDK, network, and port 8080.
primary_path.success_signal: Gradle starts downloading or resolving dependencies and launches the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrapper execution permission, proxy, repository, compilation, test, Java, or port errors can block startup.

primary_path.action: Confirm Spring Boot and embedded Tomcat started.
details: The final log lines indicate Spring has started.
Embedded Apache Tomcat listens on localhost port 8080.
primary_path.success_signal: Startup completes without an exception and port 8080 is active.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A port conflict or startup exception means the endpoint is not ready.

details: The request omits the optional name parameter, so the default World is used.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong port, route, stopped process, or local firewall prevents the request.

details: The method formats Hello and the default name with an exclamation point.
primary_path.output: Confirmed first Spring Boot application success.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Any other response indicates code, route, parameter, build, or runtime mismatch.

branches.condition: The developer uses VS Code or Eclipse.
branches.path: Install VS Code plus Spring Boot Extension Pack, or Eclipse plus Spring Tools, then import the extracted project and use the same generated source and wrapper.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds IDE-specific extension or tooling installation.

branches.path: From the project root run .\gradlew.bat bootRun instead of ./gradlew bootRun.

branches.condition: The developer selects Maven rather than the Gradle configuration implied by the quickstart run command.
branches.path: Generate the Maven project, add the same controller code, and run it with the generated Maven wrapper using the official Building an Application guide's ./mvnw spring-boot:run command.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes build files, wrapper, dependency-resolution path, and run command while keeping the endpoint response.

branches.condition: The IDE has Spring Initializr integration.
branches.path: Create the same Web project through the IDE's Initializr flow, then add the documented controller and run it.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces browser download and manual unzip with IDE-driven project generation.

friction_gates.description: A compatible JDK must be installed and visible to the shell and IDE.
The developer chooses and may need to install an IDE and extension set.
Spring Initializr exposes build tool, language, Boot version, metadata, packaging, and Java choices that the quickstart does not fully enumerate.
The project is delivered as a ZIP that must be downloaded and unpacked.
The build needs dependency repository access and permission to execute the wrapper and bind port 8080.

sources.title: Spring Quickstart Guide
sections_used: Step 1: Start a new Spring Boot project
evidence_supported: Complete super-quick path, prerequisites, endpoint code, run commands, server signal, and browser result

sources.title: Spring Boot
evidence_supported: Vendor selection of Quickstart as Super quick and the Building guide as More general

sources.title: Building an Application with Spring Boot
evidence_supported: Current Java 17+, Gradle or Maven options, Spring Web dependency name, IDE Initializr route, and Maven run branch

uncertainties.question: Which exact Spring Initializr values should accompany the quickstart's Web dependency?
uncertainties.impact: Build tool, language, package name, Java version, and metadata determine generated files, code language, and run command.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart instructs the developer to create a web project and shows the Gradle wrapper later but does not textually enumerate every current Initializr field.

uncertainties.question: Which operating system and IDE does the developer use?
uncertainties.impact: JDK, IDE, extension, archive, executable-permission, and wrapper syntax steps differ.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The guide presents multiple IDEs and macOS/Linux versus Windows commands.

uncertainties.question: What setup is required to install the chosen JDK and IDE?
uncertainties.impact: Those external installations can dominate the first-mile step count on a clean machine.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The guide recommends and links tools but does not reproduce their vendor-specific installation journeys.

excluded_after_success: Adding ?name=Amy and answering the Pop quiz.
Building REST, data, security, messaging, cloud, or other Spring guides.
Adding tests, Actuator, executable JAR packaging, container images, deployment, production configuration, security, observability, and enterprise support.
